# **Version History** {#version-history}

| Date | Author | Version | Change Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 4/14/2025 | Cyrus / David | 1.0 | Initial version |
| 5/16/2025 | Cyrus / David / Jenny | 2.0 | Updated program details and finalizing BRD |

# **Background** {#background}

## Description: {#description:}

Today, the Marketplace platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) have a manual document verification process for orders. The process to verify and collect order booking artifacts—including any accompanying documentation—is highly manual, time-consuming, and error-prone. While the platforms themselves might have varying details, the workflow to verify and process these artifacts remains largely consistent across all three platforms, which amplifies inefficiencies on a larger scale.

As demand increases and the marketplace business expands, the reliance on manual processes is causing operational bottlenecks, longer processing times, and putting a strain on cross-functional teams responsible for each step. Automating this workflow is crucial to prevent delays, reduce errors, and ensure compliance with EDI PO automation standards.

## Objectives: {#objectives:}

The main objective is to automate the collection, validation, and processing of Marketplace order verification artifacts (e.g., Order verification form (OVF), Rubrik Quote, and Marketplace order acceptance confirmation) across AWS, Azure, and GCP platforms. Specifically, the project aims to:

* Standardize and automate the order document verification process across all three platforms.  
* Eliminate manual intervention wherever possible to improve operational efficiency.  
* Ensure that all processes comply with EDI PO automation standards for regulatory accuracy.

## Business Justification: {#business-justification:}

### **Problem Statement:** {#problem-statement:}

The process of verifying and validating order-related documents across the AWS, Azure, and GCP Marketplace platforms is currently highly manual. This introduces inefficiencies, increases the risk of human error, and creates operational bottlenecks, especially as the volume of Marketplace orders grows.  
Business Case:   
 

# **Project Contacts** {#project-contacts}

| Team | Name |
| :---- | :---- |
| Order Management | [Cyrus Sharif](mailto:cyrus.sharif@rubrik.com)[David Moldvai](mailto:david.moldvai@rubrik.com) |
| Process Lead | Jenny Heng |

# **Scope** {#scope}

| Scope Is | Comments |
| :---- | :---- |
| SW only order booking process**ex.** M365 or TPH Only |  |
| SW booking with Non-Aspen HW | The order booking process is same as SW only booking |
| SW booking with Aspen HW (manually split at quoting) | The order booking process is same as SW only booking |
| Ramp order booking **ex.** GC Offer, Delayed Start, Ramp | The order booking process is the same as SW only booking. Quote is automatically flagged for manual fulfillment from the upstream process. |
| Order Booking for Scale Utility (GC Offer, Renewal, Amendment) | The order booking process is same as SW only booking |
| **Scope Is Not** |  |
| Private Offer Creation | Automating private offer creation is not in scope for any order variety. |
| HW / SW Split Opps linkage | For MP deals with HW and SW. 2 separate opps and quotes must be created and DealOps links both Opps by referencing related quotes on both Opps. Linkage is not in scope. |
| Fulfillment Processes | No changes to the fulfillment process. Orders requiring manual fulfillment today will still require manual fulfillment. |

# **Assumptions** {#assumptions}

| ID | Assumption | Function/Group |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1.0 | Upstream process including Oppty, quoting, and MP offer creation and set up will remain manual | Marketplace Team |

# **Business Requirements** {#business-requirements}

**Priority Classification:**

* **MVP/P0:** Urgent requirement; Must include   
* **P1:** Important but not urgent; Should include  
* **P2:** Desired but not important; Want to include  
* **P3:** Future consideration; Include later

### **Order Booking Requirements** {#order-booking-requirements}

| No. | Priority | Description | JIRA Story |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| ORDB-01 |  | Rubrik must receive the data (see fields below) via transmission from Reseller or Customer.  Transmission can’t come from Rubrik internal people (AE / BDM Team / Etc.) |  |
| ORDB-02 |  | Customer or Partner must send following information to Rubrik and the system must absorb info into the related field in SFDC. Required fields to be sent to Rubrik and ensure they match expectations End User Name Ship To Information Company Name Address Contact \- Name, Phone, Email Rubrik Quote PDF \- includes all necessary Product Information Part Number Quantity $ Amount Subscription Terms ~~Payment Schedule (if applicable)~~ Agreement to additional terms If terms are present on the quote, and the quote data we consume is part of this, the additional terms should be automatically accepted. Marketplace Customer ID Azure orders only \- “Waive 72 hour cancellation?” True/False |  |
| ORDB-03 |  | The following fields must be Autopopulated upon Order Creation. This info will not be provided to us by Customer or Partner. Distributor PO Number \= Marketplace Order End User PO \= Marketplace Order Payment Terms \= standard for Marketplace Carrier Information default to ‘No Shipping Required’ (no hardware is sold on Marketplace) |  |
| ORDB-04 |  | Standard Process: Internal/External Booking validations must remain consistent. e.g. Fuzzy match for End User ; quote not primary ; incorrect product name, etc**reference:** [https://rubrik.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/IS/pages/990806520/Stage+PO+validation+Code](https://rubrik.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/IS/pages/990806520/Stage+PO+validation+Code)  |  |
| ORDB-05 |  | If CRSD is provided, it must be consumed and follow existing CRSD policies |  |
| ORDB-06 |  | The system must automatically cross-validate the Proof of Marketplace Acceptance with the received order information (e.g., OVF/Quote) to ensure consistency before proceeding. See: ORDB-06 Booking Cross-Validation Documents at bottom of this document What information is validated?  AWS **OVF Buyer (End User) AWS Tenant ID** should be an exact match vs **Customer AWS Account ID** on AWS Proof of Acceptance **SFDC Quote Amount** should be an exact match vs **Purchase amount** on AWS Proof of Acceptance  Azure  **Buyer (End User) Name** should be a fuzzy 80% match vs **Company Name** on Azure Proof of Acceptance (Tackle notification) GCP **OVF Buyer (End User) GCP Billing Account** should be an exact match vs **Billing account** on GCP Proof of Acceptance **Buyer (End User) Name** should be a fuzzy 80% match vs **Customer** on GCP Proof of Acceptance **Amount match(?)** Questionable because of Telus example where Quote amount did not match Acceptance amount due to 2% cutout |  |
| ORDB-07 |  | New Internal Stage PO Validation Error for Marketplace: OVF/Quote vs Proof of Marketplace Acceptance  |  |
| ORDB-08 |  | The system must support a mechanism to check for and apply any 72-Hour Waivers specific to Azure order processing. Note: 72 hour hold does not apply to renewal orders for Azure. |  |
| ORDB-09 |  | The system must place AZURE Orders on Manual Fulfillment. |  |
| ORDB-10 |  | For AWS and GCP orders, the system must trigger the standard fulfillment process |  |
| ORDB-11 |  | New Notifications to OM:1\. When documents are received (e.g. when OVF comes in, OM should be notified) 2\. Stage PO failed to create \*Provide email templates\* |  |
| ORDB-12 |  | The system should be flexible enough to integrate with new cloud marketplaces in the future with minimal redevelopment. |  |
| ORDB-13 |  | Report for in-house Marketplace Documentation within the [PO at Rubrik](https://rubrikinc.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Report/00O1W000004xwIo/view) Report: Document 1: OVF (which includes the PDF) Document 2: Marketplace Acceptance Purpose: OM needs to be able to report on OVF / Marketplace Acceptances Use Quote as base → track documents (e.g. 1 of 2 docs received for Q-XXXXXX) |  |
| ORDB-14 |  | For other non-standard quote constructions requiring manual fulfillment (ramp, delayed start date, recontracting) order should auto book and mark the order for manual fulfillment per standard process. |  |
| ~~ORDB-15~~ |  | ~~For any payment schedule transaction (pay annual, utility) \- payment schedule should be consumed on Stage PO, validated against quote (in alignment with ENHANCEMENT) and order should auto book.~~ |  |

### **Order Provisioning Requirements** {#order-provisioning-requirements}

| ORDP-01 |  | Standard Process: Order provisioning logic should remain as-is e.g. RSC products should go through RSC provisioning ; RSC-P products do not require RSC provisioning |  |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |

### **Order Approval Requirements** {#order-approval-requirements}

| No. | Priority | Description | JIRA Story |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| ORDA-01 |  | Azure orders should enter in NS as pending approval. Once the order’s report from Azure is received ( via b2b transmission). It should proceed with validation. If the following 2 data points match, it should be automatically approved. Order Amount on Sales Order matches Order Amount on Azure’s Report *AssetID* from the **Azure** **Orders Report** matches the *Subscription ID* from the **Azure Tackle Notification (Proof of Acceptance)** If it does not auto approve, an email should be sent to [orders@rubrik.com](mailto:orders@rubrik.com). ***Email Example:*****Subject:** Azure Order Not Approved \- Order’s Report Amount Mismatch **To**: [Orders@rubrik.com](mailto:Orders@rubrik.com) ,Cyrus Sharif, Vanny Ellis  **Body:** Hi Order’s Team \- The below order we received the Azure Order’s report but it does not match based on Subscription ID or  Order Amount. Please check and validate. \<Order Number\>Thanks, NS Team.  Note: This process should account for any potential delays (e.g., 48 hours post-acceptance) in Azure report availability |  |
| ORDA-02 |  | After 72 hours (includes weekends/holidays) Azure Marketplace order is subscribed to, Manual Fulfillment Hold should be released and auto fulfill (assuming it is approved and all fulfillment criteria are met). |  |
| ORDA-03 |  | GCP and AWS orders should enter into NS as pending fulfillment (similar to EDI orders, i.e. Ingram Micro) |  |
